title: papaver alpinum subsp. corona-sancti-stephani
intro: Papaver alpinum subsp. corona-sancti-stephani, commonly known as alpine poppy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the poppy family (Papaveraceae). It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic qualities, which make it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce pain, inflammation, and fever, as well as its potential to act as an antioxidant and antimicrobial agent. Alpine poppy is typically prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and its extracts are also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
